_comment: Dependency traps and deliberate package-state policy. 'exceptions' are packages in debloat-list.json that must stay installed anyway: 01-debloat.sh SKIPs them, 02-regression-fixes.sh restores them, 99-verify-all.sh asserts them (currently empty — gallery3d was here until the camera-preview tradeoff was decided the other way, see FIELD-NOTES #4). 'mustStayEnabled' are packages NOT in the debloat list that must never be disabled/uninstalled. 'keepDisabled' are packages not in the debloat list that are deliberately disabled (pm disable-user, data preserved): 01-debloat.sh enforces, 99-verify-all.sh asserts not-enabled. Each entry records the symptom/reason, because every one of these was learned the hard way.

mustStayEnabled.reason: Owns the widget-catalog ContentProvider (com.oplus.pantanal.ums.card.configuration.provider). Disabled, the lockscreen 'Add widget' picker spins forever — PantanalClient inits with loadTimeout=-1.
Owns the Epona IPC bus used by Pantanal; same lockscreen widget-picker hang as pantanal.ums.

Lockscreen widget plugin ('InspirationWidget' in logs) that talks to Pantanal. Needed alongside pantanal.ums + uiengine — enabling it alone is not sufficient, but disabling it breaks lockscreen widgets too.

keepDisabled.reason: OPPO App Market — phones home and is the storefront surface; disabled (not uninstalled) so app data survives. Its one legitimate job, delivering download-on-demand first-party apps (IR Remote, com.oplus.consumerIRApp), is covered by scripts/fetch-apk.sh sideloading with Oplus-signature verification instead.
